title: Calydorea guatemalensis
taxonomy: Calydorea guatemalensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It belongs to the genus Calydorea, which is a group of plants native to Central America and Mexico. The classification of Calydorea guatemalensis also includes its rank as a species within the kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification is further broken down into its domain Eukarya and phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification provides insight into the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Calydorea guatemalensis, also known as the Guatemala marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Iridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Calydorea guatemalensis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Calydorea guatemalensis has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin infections, and digestive issues.
